perturbation.original_text: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep the Discloser's Confidential Information confidential and, except with the prior written consent of the Discloser or as expressly permitted by this agreement, shall: 3.1.1 not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose; 3.1.2 not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party; 3.1.3 not make any copies, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion in whole or in part except as strictly necessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Discloser); 3.1.4 keep the Confidential Information in a safe and secure place and establish and main tain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorised access or use, including but not limited to implementing any reasonable security measures proposed by the Discloser from time to time; and 3.1.5 at the written requ est of the Discloser, or in any event on termination of this agreement, return or destroy all documents or other records containing Confidential Information to the Discloser and confirm to the Discloser that it has done so in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep the Discloser's Confidential Information confidential unless required to share with their subsidiaries and, except with the prior written consent of the Discloser or as expressly permitted by this agreement, shall: 3.1.1 not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose; 3.1.2 not disclose or make available the Confidential Information in whole or in part to any third party, except for its subsidiaries; 3.1.3 not make any copies, reduce to writing or otherwise record the Confidential Informa tion in whole or in part except as strictly necessary for the Purpose (and any such copies, reductions to writing and records shall be the property of the Discloser); 3.1.4 keep the Confidential Information in a safe and secure place and establish and main tain adequate security measures to safeguard the Confidential Information from unauthorised access or use, including but not limited to implementing any reasonable security measures proposed by the Discloser from time to time; and 3.1.5 at the written requ est of the Discloser, or in any event on termination of this agreement, return or destroy all documents or other records containing Confidential Information to the Discloser and confirm to the Discloser that it has done so in writing.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the phrase 'unless required to share with their subsidiaries' and 'except for its subsidiaries' introduces an exception to the confidentiality obligation. This directly conflicts with section 4.1 which requires that each representative shall comply with this agreement as if it were the recipient, unless explicit process has been made. It's unclear whether the subsidiaries are bound by the same confidentiality terms, potentially weakening the protection of the Confidential Information. The original intent was strict, while the amended clause now introduces an undefined exception.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. PERMITTED DISCLOSURE S 4.1 The Recipient may disclose all or part of the Discloser's Confidential Information to a Representative who needs to know that particular Confidential Information for the Pur pose provided that, in relation to each Representative to whom the Discloser's Confidential Information is to be disclosed, the Recipient prior to disclosure: 4.1.1 informs the Representative of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information; 4.1.2 informs the Discloser of the identity of the Representative to whom it intends to disclose Confidential Information; 4.1.3 procures that the Representative shall, in relation to any Confidential Information disclosed, comply with this agreement as if it were the Recipient; and 4.1.4 if the Discloser so requests, in relation to a Representative other than an employee of the Recipient, procures that such Representative enters into a confidentiality agreement with the Discloser on terms equivalent to those contained in this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 3.1 introduces exceptions to the confidentiality obligation by allowing disclosure to subsidiaries, 'unless required to share with their subsidiaries' and 'except for its subsidiaries'. This directly contradicts section 4.1, which states that any representative (including subsidiaries) who receives confidential information must comply with the agreement as if they were the recipient and that the Discloser must be informed and consent to the disclosure. The added exceptions in 3.1 create ambiguity about whether subsidiaries are bound by the same strict confidentiality terms, which weakens the protection intended by 4.1 and creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, which reserves all rights in its Confidential Information. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, unless the Recipient substantially improves the information, in which case the improved portion belongs to the Recipient. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'unless the Recipient substantially improves the information, in which case the improved portion belongs to the Recipient,' section 5.1 now contradicts itself. While initially stating that all Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party, it then creates an exception where the Recipient gains ownership. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 All Confidential Information shall remain the property of th e party disclosing it, unless the Recipient substantially improves the information, in which case the improved portion belongs to the Recipient. No rights, including, but not limited to, any and all intellectual property rights, in respect of a party's Confidential Information are granted to the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 5.1 introduces a contradiction. Initially, it states that all confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party. However, it then adds an exception where the recipient gains ownership if they substantially improve the information. This directly contradicts the original statement and creates an in-text contradiction within the same section, as the contradicted text is identical to the changed text. Thus, the change breaks the initial claim, making the changed and contradicted text incompatible, according to the prompt instructions.

perturbation.original_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION If either party decides not to continue to be involved in the Purpose with the other party it shall notify the other party in writing and this agreement will terminate w ith immediate effect. The obligations of each party shall continue for a period of [ Insert number of years - this should be a realistic estimate of how long it is likely that the information that is to be disclosed will remain important to protect, e.g. 5 ] years from the termination of this agreement. Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION If either party decides not to continue to be involved in the Purpose with the other party it shall notify the other party in writing and this agreement will terminate w ith immediate effect, unless termination is due to a breach of this agreement by the Recipient, in which case the agreement continues indefinitely until the Discloser decides otherwise. The obligations of each party shall continue for a period of [ Insert number of years - this should be a realistic estimate of how long it is likely that the information that is to be disclosed will remain important to protect, e.g. 5 ] years from the termination of this agreement. Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause 'unless termination is due to a breach of this agreement by the Recipient, in which case the agreement continues indefinitely until the Discloser decides otherwise' creates a contradiction with the initial statement that the agreement terminates with immediate effect upon written notification. The duration is no longer immediately terminated, making it in conflict with the first part of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. TERM AND TERMINATION If either party decides not to continue to be involved in the Purpose with the other party it shall notify the other party in writing and this agreement will terminate w ith immediate effect, unless termination is due to a breach of this agreement by the Recipient, in which case the agreement continues indefinitely until the Discloser decides otherwise. The obligations of each party shall continue for a period of [ Insert number of years - this should be a realistic estimate of how long it is likely that the information that is to be disclosed will remain important to protect, e.g. 5 ] years from the termination of this agreement. Termination of this agreement shall not affect any accrued rights or remedies to which either party is entitled.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7 to state that the agreement terminates immediately upon written notification, *unless* termination is due to a breach by the Recipient, in which case it continues indefinitely. The contradicted text is the same modified Section 7. The original Section 7 stated that any termination would be with immediate effect, but the new text creates an exception to that, creating a direct conflict within the document. The explanation confirms this by stating that the duration is no longer immediately terminated, making it in conflict with the first part of the section. This is a clear instance of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, since a key term (termination) is defined inconsistently within the same section.
